mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p<0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and rising recession probability, causing institutional investors to de-risk and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of months where UNRATE > 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer spending and real estate investment, creating a positive feedback loop that lifts corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Interaction term between RSAFS and HOUST growth shows negative or zero predictive power for 20-day returns in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates into higher short-term equity returns as markets price in improved operational efficiency.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict 5-day forward returns with a rank IC above 0.02 during periods of stable inventory levels.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 195.0, persistent inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in >70% of observations where CPIAUCSL > 195.0.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates signals a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer spending power, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline shows no statistically significant relationship with quarterly equity returns (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly correlates with aggregate corporate profit expansion, providing a persistent positive drift to S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter as earnings reports confirm macroeconomic strength.
failure_condition: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth exhibits a negative or zero correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls below 105.0, manufacturing contraction signals weakening economic activity and reduced capacity utilization, prompting short-term sell-offs in equity markets over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where INDPRO < 105.0, contradicting the contraction signal.
